item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This started off really strong. The hook and initial anomaly are excellent, and I love the image of this kid just repeatedly cycling into ice cream vans for seemingly no reason. I was immensely disappointed when it became a generic wish-fulfilling story, with the entertaining premise all but entirely thrown out of the window in favour of the same 'person wishes for family/other nice thing over riches' that's present in almost all fiction involving genie-like characters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may be a touch longer than it needs to be, but I really like the story here. Gave me some strong Spirited Away vibes at times, in a good way.
Well. I honestly didn't even expect to get 30. Thanks to everybody who upvoted, and I'm glad you all enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deal-making, wish-granting supernatural beings are hard to do well. This was done quite well. I'm curious about -1's bosses, which I'm sure was an intended outcome.

All in all, very satisfying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta say, when I saw the title, I didn't think a story about ice cream was going to be as good as this. Pleasantly surprised.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the nice comment! I'm glad you though the containment procedures were compelling! (Goodness knows it took awhile to get those written, haha…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got the feeling he's more some kind of demon, likely something from myth. I ended the article wanting to know what he was, exactly, but not knowing didn't diminish it in my eyes.

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded: Iteration after iteration, and it steadily increased in quality with each one. Great job :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm. I liked the article up until the point where it was revealed that 3870-1 was a genie of some kind - the interview log didn't provide any insight into a compelling character and I was much more enthralled by the idea of this kid's Stand indiscriminately beating up ice cream vendors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a perfect example of how to tell a story in this format. The containment procedures alone draw you in and make you go, "Oh my god what is this?" This is top-tier stuff!

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, what a process. Big, big thanks to everyone in chat who read over this, special thanks to Hippo and Kirby who spent a fair amount of time hashing out the concepts in the final stages of the draft. Also special shout-out to Kid F who read this in the forums!

This sort of anomaly is something new for me, and all the suggestions we got during the editing stage were much appreciated. Here's hoping that readers enjoy the work!

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded: After many different ideas and attempts, here's my first SCP. This was co-authored by Zyn (Thanks for putting up with me through this) and reviewed by a lot of people. Thanks to not_a_seagull, taylor_itkin, lavenderbloodstains, Roserado, Neltharak, CaptainKirby, MadLeod and Kid F for looking at it and offering feedback. Special thanks to Hippo and Kirby for helping us during the final stages of the draft and for offering thoughts on the draft as a whole. This wouldn’t have been possible without everybody who offered feedback, thank you all once again.
